name: Drone Hover Power Calculator
description: Estimate the power required for a drone to hover based on its weight and rotor size.
formulaDescription: Ideal hover power (P) is calculated from the required thrust (T), air density (ρ), and total rotor disk area (A).

example.explanation: A 1.2kg drone (11.77N total weight) requires 2.943N thrust per motor. With 9-inch props, it needs ~165W total power to hover.

faqs.question: Does larger propeller size reduce hover power?
faqs.answer: Yes, larger propellers have a larger disk area, which reduces 'disk loading'. Lower disk loading is more efficient and requires less power for the same amount of thrust.

faqs.question: How does air density affect hover power?
faqs.answer: At higher altitudes (lower air density), the drone must spin its propellers faster to generate the same thrust, which increases power consumption.

concept: Hover power is the baseline energy consumption required just to stay airborne against gravity.
guideSteps: Enter the total 'take-off' weight of your drone (mass in kg).
Enter the number of rotors (usually 4 for quadcopters).
Enter the diameter of the propellers used (in inches).
The calculator will display the power needed for a steady hover.
